title: Centre Tightens FCRA Rules: NGOs Must Declare Social Media Accounts, Political Content Barred
summary: The Central Government has amended the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA) rules, mandating that NGOs receiving foreign funds must declare their social media accounts to the authorities. The amended rules also restrict NGOs to activities specified in their registration and bar them from engaging in or publishing political content. This is part of a broader effort to tighten oversight of foreign-funded civil society organisations in India.
exam_angle: FCRA is a key legislation governing foreign contributions to Indian organisations; amendments reflect the government's continued scrutiny of civil society funding and are relevant to GS Paper 2 topics on governance, transparency, and civil society.
key_facts: Act involved: Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Act (FCRA)
New requirement: NGOs must declare social media accounts
Restriction: NGOs must stick to specified activities; political content barred
Implementing authority: Ministry of Home Affairs

title: Supreme Court Rules Collegium Decisions Are Beyond Judicial Review
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that decisions of the collegium system for appointment and elevation of judges are beyond judicial review, reaffirming that courts will not interfere in the judge selection process. This ruling reinforces the primacy of the collegium in judicial appointments and shields the process from external challenges. The collegium system, comprising senior-most judges, has been the subject of longstanding debate regarding transparency and accountability.
exam_angle: The collegium system and its constitutional basis (Articles 124, 217) are frequently examined topics; this ruling reinforces the 'Three Judges Cases' precedent and is directly relevant to judicial appointments and independence.
key_facts: Collegium system established through: Three Judges Cases (1981, 1993, 1998)
Relevant Articles: 124 (Supreme Court appointments), 217 (High Court appointments)
Ruling: Collegium decisions are non-justiciable (beyond judicial review)
Context: Reaffirms judiciary's exclusive control over judge selection

title: India-China Hold 'Constructive' Talks at BRICS NSA Meeting
summary: India's Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) highlighted that discussions between India and China at the BRICS National Security Advisors (NSA) meeting were 'constructive'. The bilateral talks are part of ongoing diplomatic engagement following the 2020 Galwan Valley standoff and subsequent disengagement process along the Line of Actual Control (LAC). The BRICS NSA-level meeting provides a multilateral platform for India-China engagement.
exam_angle: India-China relations and BRICS are perennial GS Paper 2 (IR) topics; NSA-level talks signal high-level engagement and connect to the LAC disengagement process and India's multilateral diplomacy.
key_facts: Platform: BRICS NSA (National Security Advisors) Meeting
MEA described talks as: 'constructive'
Context: Ongoing India-China diplomatic normalisation post-2020 LAC standoff
BRICS members: Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa (+ new members from 2024)

title: India-US Ministerial-Level Trade Pact Talks Held; US Trade Chief Greer Visits India
summary: India and the United States held ministerial-level talks on a bilateral trade agreement, with US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer visiting India to resolve outstanding hurdles in the trade pact negotiations. The US Embassy stated that Washington is focused on a 'fair trade deal' that benefits both countries. India's government is simultaneously seeking a 'comparative advantage' in the deal. The negotiations are set against the backdrop of US tariff pressures and the broader Indo-Pacific economic engagement.
exam_angle: India-US trade relations, bilateral trade agreements, and the US tariff regime are critical GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy) topics; this development is also relevant to India's export competitiveness and trade diplomacy.
key_facts: US Trade Representative: Jamieson Greer visited India
Level of talks: Ministerial-level
US stated goal: 'Fair trade deal benefiting both countries'
India's stated goal: 'Comparative advantage' in the trade deal

title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns
summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer of the Labour Party has announced his resignation as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom. Starmer had come to power in July 2024 after Labour's landslide election victory, ending 14 years of Conservative rule. His resignation raises questions about political stability in the UK and the future direction of British domestic and foreign policy.
exam_angle: Changes in government leadership of major democracies are relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) and current affairs; UK-India bilateral relations (including the Free Trade Agreement negotiations) may be impacted.
key_facts: Resigned PM: Keir Starmer (Labour Party)
Starmer came to power: July 2024 (general election victory)
UK is a permanent member of the UN Security Council and G7 nation
India-UK Free Trade Agreement negotiations were ongoing under Starmer's tenure

title: India-Israel Identify New Joint Defence Projects to Build Bilateral Resilience
summary: India and Israel have identified new joint defence projects aimed at building bilateral resilience, deepening their strategic defence partnership. The two countries have a longstanding defence cooperation relationship, with Israel being one of India's top defence suppliers. The new projects are expected to focus on co-development and co-production of defence technologies.
exam_angle: India-Israel defence ties are important for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Defence); connects to India's policy of Atmanirbhar Bharat in defence and its broader West Asia foreign policy.
key_facts: Countries involved: India and Israel
Focus: Joint defence co-development and co-production projects
Israel is among India's top 3 defence suppliers
Relevant policy: Atmanirbhar Bharat in defence; India-Israel Strategic Partnership

title: US Approves $482 Million Military Support Package for India's Apache Helicopters and M777 Howitzers
summary: The United States government has approved a $482 million military support package for India, covering maintenance and support for Apache attack helicopters and M777 ultra-light howitzers already in service with the Indian armed forces. This approval comes under the Foreign Military Sales (FMS) mechanism. The package underscores the deepening US-India defence partnership under the Major Defence Partnership framework.
exam_angle: US-India defence deals under the FMS route are a recurring exam topic; Apache helicopters and M777 howitzers are part of India's recent defence modernisation and are connected to the LEMOA, COMCASA, and BECA foundational agreements.
key_facts: Package value: USD 482 million
Platforms covered: Apache attack helicopters, M777 ultra-light howitzers
Route: Foreign Military Sales (FMS)
Framework: US-India Major Defence Partnership

title: India Becomes World's Top Ship Recycling Nation in 2025
summary: India has emerged as the world's leading ship recycling nation in 2025, overtaking Bangladesh and Pakistan. The Alang ship-breaking yard in Gujarat is the world's largest ship recycling facility and has been central to this achievement. Ship recycling is a significant industry for India in terms of employment and recovery of recyclable materials, though it also raises environmental and worker safety concerns.
exam_angle: India's maritime economy and ship recycling industry are relevant to GS Paper 3 (Economy/Environment); Alang yard and the Hong Kong Convention on ship recycling are potential exam facts.
key_facts: India ranked: World's No. 1 ship recycling nation in 2025
Key facility: Alang ship recycling yard, Gujarat
Competitors: Bangladesh, Pakistan
Relevant convention: Hong Kong International Convention for Safe and Environmentally Sound Recycling of Ships

title: India Business Activity Slows to Three-Month Low in June; Optimism Dips to Six-Month Low
summary: India's composite business activity index, tracked through the Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI), slowed to a three-month low in June 2026, while business optimism fell to a six-month low. The data, published by Moneycontrol, suggests a moderation in economic momentum and could influence the Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy considerations. Both manufacturing and services sectors contributed to the slowdown.
exam_angle: PMI data is a leading economic indicator frequently referenced in economy current affairs; relevant to RBI monetary policy decisions and GS Paper 3 (Economy) topics on growth, inflation, and business cycles.
key_facts: Indicator: Composite PMI (Purchasing Managers' Index)
June 2026 reading: Three-month low for business activity
Business optimism: Six-month low
Implication: May influence RBI rate decisions

title: Sensex Crashes ~900 Points on Global Sell-Off; IT and Banking Stocks Drag Markets
summary: BSE Sensex fell by approximately 893–900 points and Nifty slipped below 23,850 on June 23, 2026, driven by a global equity sell-off. IT and banking stocks were the primary drags on the indices. Key factors cited include global risk-off sentiment, concerns over US trade negotiations, and weak economic data. The fall reflects the sensitivity of Indian markets to global developments.
exam_angle: Stock market movements linked to global factors, sectoral composition of Sensex/Nifty, and the role of FII flows are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy) and financial markets topics.
key_facts: Sensex fall: ~893–900 points on June 23, 2026
Nifty level: Slipped below 23,850
Key drags: IT and banking sector stocks
Trigger: Global sell-off and risk-off sentiment

title: IRDAI Mandates Naming of Sellers in Insurance Policies Distributed via Banks and Agents
summary: The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) has directed that insurance policies sold through banks (bancassurance) and agents must name the individual seller on the policy document. This regulatory change aims to improve accountability and transparency in the distribution of insurance products and protect consumer interests. The move is part of IRDAI's broader reforms to strengthen insurance penetration and consumer protection.
exam_angle: IRDAI regulations, insurance distribution reforms, and bancassurance are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy) and financial sector regulation; connects to India's goal of 'Insurance for All by 2047'.
key_facts: Regulator: IRDAI (Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India)
New rule: Policies sold via banks/agents must name the individual seller
Distribution channel: Bancassurance and individual agents
Goal: Improve accountability and consumer protection

title: India to Open PSLV Rocket Technology to Private Firms in Push for Space Launch Hub
summary: India has announced plans to open Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) rocket technology to private companies, as part of the government's strategy to develop India into a global space launch hub. This follows the liberalisation of the space sector under the Indian Space Policy 2023 and the establishment of IN-SPACe (Indian National Space Promotion and Authorisation Centre). The move is expected to boost the nascent private space launch industry in India.
exam_angle: ISRO's PSLV technology transfer to private players is a landmark development in India's space sector liberalisation; connects to IN-SPACe, Indian Space Policy 2023, and the broader GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) space economy topic.
key_facts: Technology being opened: PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) rocket technology
Regulatory body for private space: IN-SPACe (Indian National Space Promotion and Authorisation Centre)
Policy framework: Indian Space Policy 2023
Goal: Make India a global space launch hub

title: Meta Invests $900 Million in CRED; Appoints Kunal Shah as Global Head of WhatsApp
summary: US tech giant Meta has announced a $900 million investment in Indian fintech startup CRED and has appointed CRED's founder Kunal Shah as the Global Head of WhatsApp. This marks one of the largest investments by a global tech company in an Indian startup and signals Meta's deepening interest in India's digital payments and financial services ecosystem. Kunal Shah, who founded CRED in 2018, becomes one of the highest-profile Indian tech entrepreneurs to lead a global product division.
exam_angle: Foreign direct investment in Indian startups, digital payments ecosystem, and the role of Indian tech entrepreneurs in global companies are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy/Sci-Tech); connects to India's startup ecosystem and digital economy.
key_facts: Investment: Meta invests $900 million in CRED (Indian fintech startup)
Appointment: Kunal Shah (CRED founder) named Global Head of WhatsApp
CRED founded: 2018 by Kunal Shah
Significance: One of the largest global tech investments in an Indian startup

title: Five Eyes Intelligence Alliance Warns AI Models Capable of Devastating Cyber Attacks Are Months Away
summary: In a rare joint statement, the Five Eyes intelligence alliance — comprising the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand — warned that AI models capable of launching devastating cyberattacks on governments and businesses could be available within months. The statement highlights growing concerns about the weaponisation of AI and the risks posed to critical national infrastructure. This is one of the first joint AI security warnings issued by the Five Eyes grouping.
exam_angle: AI-related security threats and the Five Eyes alliance are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech/Security) and GS Paper 2 (IR); connects to cybersecurity, critical infrastructure protection, and international AI governance debates.
key_facts: Issuing body: Five Eyes alliance (US, UK, Canada, Australia, New Zealand)
Warning: AI models capable of devastating cyberattacks 'months away'
Target of concern: Governments and business critical infrastructure
Significance: Rare joint AI security statement from Five Eyes

title: Monsoon Enters Mumbai After Delay; IMD Issues Heavy Rain Warning; Recent Rains in Some Areas Not Classified as Monsoon
summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) confirmed that the southwest monsoon has finally entered Mumbai after a significant delay in 2026, and issued warnings for heavy rainfall. Simultaneously, IMD clarified that recent rains in some parts of India — including a dust storm that hit Delhi-NCR — do not constitute monsoon onset, as large parts of India are still awaiting monsoon arrival. IMD issued an orange alert for parts of NCR and a red alert for Delhi.
exam_angle: Southwest monsoon onset and IMD alerts are standard GS Paper 1 (Geography) topics; normal onset dates, IMD colour-coded alert system, and monsoon's significance for Indian agriculture are key exam points.
key_facts: Normal onset of monsoon in Mumbai: Around June 11
IMD alert for Delhi-NCR: Orange alert (dust storm); Red alert (rain)
IMD clarified: Pre-monsoon rains ≠ monsoon onset in non-entered areas
IMD colour-coded alerts: Green (watch), Yellow (alert), Orange (be prepared), Red (take action)

title: Europe Faces Record-Breaking Heatwave; 20 Drown in France Amid Extreme Heat
summary: An intensifying heatwave is threatening record-breaking temperatures across Europe, with France reporting 20 drowning deaths as people sought relief by swimming in unsupervised areas. The heatwave is attributed to climate change-driven extreme weather events, which have become more frequent and intense across the continent. Europe has experienced several record heat events in recent years, raising concerns about adaptation and public health.
exam_angle: Heatwaves and climate change-driven extreme weather events are key GS Paper 3 (Environment) topics; relevant to IPCC findings, heat action plans, and global climate commitments under the Paris Agreement.
key_facts: Event: Intensifying heatwave across Europe, June 2026
France: 20 deaths by drowning in unsupervised areas due to heatwave
Cause: Climate change-driven extreme heat events
Relevance: Connects to IPCC warnings on increased frequency of extreme heat

title: CBI Arrests Senior Haryana IAS Officer in Rs 60-Crore IDFC First Bank Scam
summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has arrested a senior Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer from Haryana in connection with an alleged Rs 60-crore scam involving IDFC First Bank. The arrest underscores ongoing anti-corruption enforcement actions by central investigative agencies against serving bureaucrats. Details of the specific charges and the officer's role are under investigation.
exam_angle: CBI's jurisdiction over central government employees and IAS officers, anti-corruption mechanisms, and the Delhi Special Police Establishment Act are relevant GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance) topics.
key_facts: Investigating agency: CBI (Central Bureau of Investigation)
Accused: Senior Haryana IAS officer
Alleged scam amount: Rs 60 crore
Bank involved: IDFC First Bank

title: Supreme Court Stays Delhi High Court Order for Early Release of 1993 Bowbazar Blast Convict
summary: The Supreme Court of India has stayed a Delhi High Court order that had directed the early release of a convict in the 1993 Bowbazar blast case. The Supreme Court's intervention prevents the convict's premature release pending further examination of the matter. The 1993 Bowbazar blast was a serial bomb explosion case in Kolkata.
exam_angle: Supreme Court's appellate jurisdiction over High Court orders (Article 136), powers of remission, and criminal justice matters are relevant for GS Paper 2 (Polity); also connects to discussions on early release and remission of sentences.
key_facts: Court: Supreme Court stayed Delhi High Court order
Case: 1993 Bowbazar blast (Kolkata)
Action: Stay on early/premature release of convict
Relevant provision: Article 136 (Special Leave Petition to Supreme Court)
